channel.title: Posts on Kevin McKee
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Kevin McKee

item.title: A Restricted Boltzmann Machine in R
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: github source for this project I’ve taken an interest in a succession of binary network models: the hopfield network, Boltzmann Machine, the Restricted Boltzmann Machine (RBM), and the Helmholtz Machine. There isn’t much implementation of these in R, but I did find this tutorial. Each model in some way learns the joint probability distribution of many binary variables using binary hidden nodes. The difference between the RBM and the Helmholtz machine is the former uses a kind of Gibbs sampling of the hidden states to compute gradients of node weights, whereas the ladder is a generative network that learns by “dreaming” fake data during the sleep phase of the wake-sleep algorithm.

item.title: Painting a harpsichord lid
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: Some process photos from my painting on a harpsichord lid. (I’m using this post to figure out getting images into blog posts and such.) I painted this harpsichord lid for a commission in 2016. I started with a photoshop mockup: The first stages of the actual painting are interesting to look back on because they show how fundamental color and movement are to the composition: Final result:

item.title: Art
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: Art gallery now works. Woohoo! Check out the paintings
